channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Rights groups urge Thailand to release Vietnamese activist, saying he faces torture if extradited
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — A group of nearly three dozen rights groups called on Thailand's prime minister Friday to release a Vietnamese activist who has been ordered extradited home to face imprisonment on terrorism charges, saying he faces the possibility of torture if returned.
item.dc:creator: DAVID RISING, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Harris will campaign with the Obamas later this month in Georgia and Michigan
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Vice President Kamala Harris will make her first campaign appearances with Barack Obama and Michelle Obama, two of the most popular figures in the Democratic Party, at get-out-the-vote events this month in Georgia and Michigan.
item.dc:creator: DARLENE SUPERVILLE, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Texas Supreme Court halts execution of man in shaken baby case after lawmakers' last-minute appeal
item.description: HUNTSVILLE, Texas (AP) — The Texas Supreme Court halted Thursday night’s scheduled execution of a man who would have become the first person in the U.S. put to death for a murder conviction tied to a diagnosis of shaken baby syndrome.
item.dc:creator: JUAN A. LOZANO and MICHAEL GRACZYK, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Israel needs to commit to Gaza rebuilding: Gen. David Petraeus
item.description: A lasting peace will depend on the will of the Israeli coalition government, said the retired Army general and former CIA director.
item.dc:creator: Rich Johnson, NewsNation

item.title: Murder trial to begin in small Indiana town in 2017 killings of two teenage girls
item.description: DELPHI, Ind. (AP) — A murder trial in the 2017 killings of two teenage girls is set to begin Friday in the small Indiana town where the teens and the man charged with killing them all lived. Richard Allen, 52, is accused of killing 13-year-old Abigail Williams and 14-year-old Liberty German.
item.dc:creator: RICK CALLAHAN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Alabama executes man who killed 5 and asked to be put to death
item.description: ATMORE, Ala. (AP) — Alabama executed a man Thursday who admitted to killing five people with an ax and gun during a drug-fueled rampage in 2016 and dropped his appeals and asked to be put to death. Derrick Dearman, 36, was pronounced dead at 6:14 p.m. Thursday at Holman prison in southern Alabama.
item.dc:creator: BY KIM CHANDLER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: WATCH: Nevada US Senate Debate: Jacky Rosen and Sam Brown
item.description: As the seconds tick away until Election Day on Nov. 5, Democratic incumbent Sen. Jacky Rosen and Republican challenger Sam Brown are set to debate the issues Nevadans are most concerned about heading into 2025 and beyond.
item.dc:creator: Justin Walker, NewsNation

item.title: Almost 23% of people say politics influences where they live: Poll
item.description: "Some go so far as to consider moving to find that alignment," Danielle Hale, chief economist for Realtor.com, said.
item.dc:creator: Cassie Buchman, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Colorado gold mine where tour guide was killed and tourists trapped ordered closed by regulators
item.description: DENVER (AP) — A former Colorado gold mine where a tour guide was killed and a group of tourists was trapped for hours after an elevator accident has been ordered to remain closed and not conduct tours while its operations are reviewed, state regulators said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN SLEVIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Missouri 7-year-old crashes one school bus into another during apparent training
item.description: Based on information gathered by NewsNation affiliate WDAF, it appears the bus driver may have been trying to teach the kids what to do in an emergency.
item.dc:creator: Dave D'Marko, NewsNation

item.title: Oklahoma parents and teachers sue to stop top education official's classroom Bible mandate
item.description: OKLAHOMA CITY (AP) — A group of Oklahoma parents of public school students, teachers and ministers filed a lawsuit Thursday seeking to stop the state's top education official from forcing schools to incorporate the Bible into lesson plans for students in grades 5 through 12.
item.dc:creator: SEAN MURPHY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: How a Florida retreat helps veterans struggling with PTSD
item.description: Post-traumatic stress disorder impacts thousands of Florida veterans, and one retreat has a unique approach to helping them live with their struggles and begin the healing process.
item.dc:creator: Walt Buteau, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Liam Payne died from 'multiple traumas,' 'internal and external bleeding,' autopsy reveals
item.description: Officials in Argentina have released a cause of death for Liam Payne, the former One Direction member who died on Wednesday at the age of 31.
item.dc:creator: Christine Samra, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Illinois alderwoman claims she was arrested after criticizing the mayor
item.description: Alderwoman Colby Chapman said she spent five hours in jail, in what she calls a politically motivated abuse of power.
item.dc:creator: Gabriel Castillo, NewsNation

item.title: Universal Orlando announces Epic Universe opening date
item.description: The theme park will feature more than 50 attractions, as well as entertainment, dining and shopping options across five “expansive worlds.
item.dc:creator: Scott Gustin, NewsNation

item.title: 17 dogs found living in truck, Michigan deputies say
item.description: The Kent County Animal Shelter said that it's situations like these that start small and often compound to get much worse over time.
item.dc:creator: Adriana Doria, NewsNation

item.title: Syracuse University suspends fraternity after 'repugnant' hazing video surfaces online
item.description: Video of an alleged hazing incident at Syracuse University went viral online this week. The school has since launched an investigation and suspended a fraternity.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: A father and son are both indicted on murder charges in a mass school shooting in Georgia
item.description: A grand jury in Georgia has indicted a father and son in a mass shooting at Apalachee High School in Winder.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Amy, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Homeland Security grants temporary status to Lebanese already in the United States
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Biden administration on Thursday announced temporary legal status for Lebanese citizens already in the United States, likely pleasing some Arab voters, including in the battleground state of Michigan.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: BLM's Western Solar Plan is 'fantasy world,' relies heavily on taxpayer dollars: GOP congressman
item.description: Rep. Matt Rosendale, R-Mont., said the Bureau of Land Management's updated Western Solar Plan would have to be subsidized by American taxpayers to work.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Catholic Archdiocese of LA to pay $880 million in childhood sex abuse claims
item.description: "I am sorry for every one of these incidents, from the bottom of my heart," Archbishop Jose H. Gomez wrote.
item.dc:creator: Josh DuBose, NewsNation

item.title: 7-year-old crashes one school bus into another during apparent training
item.description: She won't be able to drive for another nine years but parents of an Independence second grader say she's already had her first crash.
item.dc:creator: Dave D'Marko, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Liam Payne's family releases statement as more details, autopsy results emerge in singer's death
item.description: Payne, 31, died after a fall from the third story of the Casa Sur hotel in Buenos Aires on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: La Nina could soon arrive. Here's what that means for winter weather
item.description: The National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration’s Climate Prediction Center says there is a 60% chance that a weak La Nina event will develop this autumn and could last until March.
item.dc:creator: ISABELLA O'MALLEY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Asian American evangelicals' theology is conservative. But that doesn't mean they vote that way
item.description: The Rev. Wayne Lee leads an English-speaking church of second- and third-generation Chinese Americans in the heart of Philadelphia’s Chinatown.
item.dc:creator: DEEPA BHARATH, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Vance says 'no' when asked if Trump lost in 2020
item.description: The answer was the most specific Vance has been on the subject, and a departure from the series of evasions he has offered about the election Trump continues to say without evidence was decided fraudulently.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Brightest supermoon rising this week, and it won't be alone
item.description: Get ready to look up for the year’s biggest and brightest supermoon. And it's pairing up with a comet for a rare stargazing two-for-one.
item.dc:creator: Dominique Jack, KTLA 5

item.title: Dollar General employee finds puppies dumped in trash amid cold Arkansas weather
item.description: Three puppies, less than a week old, were abandoned this week inside of a Dollar General Market dumpster, according to Arkansas authorities.
item.dc:creator: Elena Ramirez, KRON4

item.title: 3 dead, several injured after bridge collapses in Mississippi
item.description: Three people have died and several others were injured after a bridge collapsed in Mississippi on Wednesday, according to authorities.
item.dc:creator: Cameron Smith, KRON4

item.title: After 36 years, accused killer in Oregon cold case to stand trial
item.description: An autopsy revealed Deborah Atrops was physically assaulted and strangled to death. She was 30. Then, the case went cold.
item.dc:creator: Tim Steele, ABC 4

item.title: Federal money to help states hit by hurricanes Helene and Milton nears $2 billion
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The federal government has approved nearly $2 billion in assistance across six states to help people hit hard by hurricanes Helene and Milton, the White House said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: REBECCA SANTANA, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Minnesota school district bans officer from teaching after he reenacted George Floyd's murder
item.description: MINNEAPOLIS (AP) — A Minnesota school district has banned a police officer from working as a substitute teacher after a series of “racially harmful” actions that officials say included putting a student on the ground for a reenactment of the 2020 murder of George Floyd by a Minneapolis officer.
item.dc:creator: BY STEVE KARNOWSKI, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Navy finds wreckage of fighter jet that crashed during training in Washington state
item.description: The U.S. Navy located the wreckage Wednesday of a fighter jet that crashed in Washington state during a routine training flight in mountainous terrain. The two crew members onboard remain missing.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: FEMA workers threatened by armed group in Tennessee
item.description: "They were all armed, open carry not guns drawn, but they had surrounded them and there was a lady there that was yelling and threatening them.
item.dc:creator: Karen Jenkins, ABC 4

item.title: Prosecutors will not file criminal charges against 2 people at center of Los Angeles racism scandal
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — The Los Angeles city attorney’s office said Tuesday that it does not plan to file criminal charges against two people who were investigated in connection with the unlawful recording of a racist conversation that rocked City Hall and prompted the city council president to resign af
item.dc:creator: STEFANIE DAZIO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Sewage from Mexico affects recreational opportunities north of the border
item.description: The trails leading in and out of the Tijuana River Valley Regional Park Campground are closed because water tainted with raw sewage continues to flow onto trails used by those on foot and on horseback.
item.dc:creator: Salvador Rivera, Fox 5

item.title: Lilly Ledbetter, equal pay trailblazer, dies at 86
item.description: Lilly Ledbetter, an activist for equal pay whose legal fight against her employer paved the way for the Fair Pay Act, has died. She was 86.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, Fox 5
